task_completion.description: Agent logs into the Rancher interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher login page at https://localhost with credentials being entered, or the Rancher dashboard after a successful login.

task_completion.description: Agent opens and reads the QoS policy document.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows a text editor, file viewer, or terminal displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Desktop/qos_policy.md'.

task_completion.description: Agent reconfigures the resource requests and limits for the auth-service deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI deployment edit page or a terminal (e.g., kubectl edit/apply) modifying the CPU and Memory requests/limits for the 'auth-service' deployment.

task_completion.description: Agent reconfigures the resource requests and limits for the payment-api deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI deployment edit page or a terminal modifying the CPU and Memory requests/limits for the 'payment-api' deployment.

task_completion.description: Agent reconfigures the resource requests and limits for the data-warehouse-sync deployment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI deployment edit page or a terminal modifying the CPU and Memory requests/limits for the 'data-warehouse-sync' deployment.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies that the pods are in the Running state and have the correct QoS class.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshot shows the Rancher UI pod details or terminal output (e.g., kubectl get pods -o yaml) confirming the pods for the modified deployments are 'Running' and displaying their '.status.qosClass'.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rancher web interface or genuine kubectl commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real web browser navigating Rancher or a real terminal executing kubectl commands, not a simulated environment or text file.

integrity.description: Agent opened the actual QoS policy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the file '/home/ga/Desktop/qos_policy.md' being opened, rather than the agent guessing or hallucinating the policy.

integrity.description: Agent applied changes to the actual Kubernetes cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show confirmation of deployments being updated in Rancher or successful 'deployment.apps modified' messages in the terminal, not just unapplied local YAML edits.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target deployments are located in the 'critical-path' and 'background-tasks' namespaces. The QoS policy document is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/qos_policy.md'. A minimum score of 75 is required to pass.
